summary: Partially decompiled Dolphin DSP support by adding src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c and dsp.h, moving several small DSP mail/init helpers out of assembly, wiring the new C object into obj_files.mk, and replacing anonymous func_/lbl_ references across DSP, AX, and CARD assembly with SDK-style DSP names. The PR also added basic integer pointer/size typedefs needed for hardware-register access and introduced a volatile mapped __DSPRegs array plus HW_REG macro. Evidence is limited to title and diff; there were no PR body notes or review comments.
what_changed: Added src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c with C implementations for DSPCheckMailToDSP, DSPCheckMailFromDSP, DSPReadMailFromDSP, DSPSendMailToDSP, and DSPCheckInit.

Added DSPInit in dsp.c with a C version guarded under NON_MATCHING and an inline asm fallback for the matching implementation; the comment says it is non-matching due to externs and links a decomp.me scratch.
Added src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.h declaring the DSP mail/init APIs, defining HW_REG(reg, type), and mapping volatile u16 __DSPRegs[] to address 0xCC005000.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.o before the remaining DSP assembly objects.
Removed the now-decompiled helper routines from asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s while keeping and renaming remaining assembly routines such as DSPAddTask and DSPAssertTask.

Renamed cross-file assembly calls from anonymous func_ symbols to SDK-style DSP names in AX.s, CARD.s, dsp.s, and dsp_task.s, including DSPSendMailToDSP, DSPInit, DSPAddTask, DSPAssertTask, DSPCheckMailFromDSP, and DSPReadMailFromDSP.

Renamed several DSP sbss labels in assembly/commented variable declarations from lbl_804D72xx names to semantic globals such as __DSP_init_flag, __DSP_rude_task_pending, __DSP_rude_task, __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_first_task, and __DSP_curr_task.
Added intptr_t, uintptr_t, and size_t typedefs to include/dolphin/types.h.

Changed include/functions.h prototypes/comments to use named DSP functions and added concrete prototypes for __DSP_debug_printf, OSDisableInterrupts, OSRestoreInterrupts, __OSSetInterruptHandler, and __OSUnmaskInterrupts.
smart_moves: Matched the simplest hardware-mailbox accessors in C first, using volatile 16-bit register reads/writes to preserve direct MMIO behavior.
Kept DSPInit buildable and matching by placing the intended C version under NON_MATCHING while retaining the exact inline assembly body as the active implementation.
Propagated new DSP symbol names into dependent AX and CARD assembly call sites, improving global searchability without requiring those modules to be decompiled first.
Introduced semantic names for DSP task scheduler globals, making later work in dsp_task.s easier to reason about.
Added the C object without removing still-unmatched assembly objects, enabling incremental decompilation of the DSP subsystem.
decomp_lessons: For hardware subsystems, start by matching small MMIO accessors and provide a header with explicit volatile register mapping before tackling larger scheduler/interrupt routines.

When a function is logically understood but does not match because of externs or other compiler-sensitive details, a NON_MATCHING C implementation plus exact asm fallback documents intent while preserving the build.
Rename all known call sites after identifying SDK functions; even assembly-only neighbors become more readable and future matching efforts get better anchors.
Semantic sbss/global names are especially valuable in interrupt/task code because they clarify state transitions without changing codegen.
Adding uintptr_t/intptr_t/size_t typedef support can be necessary before introducing pointer-to-address MMIO macros in decompiled Dolphin code.
naming_conventions: Dolphin DSP public-style functions use DSP-prefixed CamelCase names: DSPCheckMailToDSP, DSPCheckMailFromDSP, DSPReadMailFromDSP, DSPSendMailToDSP, DSPInit, DSPCheckInit, DSPAddTask, DSPAssertTask.

Internal DSP globals use a double-underscore __DSP_ prefix with descriptive snake_case suffixes: __DSP_init_flag, __DSP_curr_task, __DSP_first_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_rude_task, __DSP_rude_task_pending.
The DSP interrupt handler keeps the double-underscore internal name __DSPHandler.
The hardware register mapping symbol is named __DSPRegs and is declared volatile.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced anonymous branch targets/calls in assembly with resolved symbol names while preserving instruction bytes and addresses.
Used inline asm for DSPInit to keep a matching active function while preserving a nearby C draft for future cleanup.
Used a volatile halfword register array at 0xCC005000 and direct high/low halfword operations to model DSP mailbox registers.
DSPCheckMailToDSP and DSPCheckMailFromDSP match the mailbox-ready bit extraction by reading 0xCC005000 or 0xCC005004 and shifting/masking bit 15.
DSPReadMailFromDSP combines two 16-bit DSP registers into a 32-bit mail value with (__DSPRegs[2] << 16) | __DSPRegs[3].
DSPSendMailToDSP splits a 32-bit mail word into high and low 16-bit writes to __DSPRegs[0] and __DSPRegs[1].
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match DSPInit after the extern-related NON_MATCHING issue.
Find follow-up PRs decompiling dsp_task.s functions around __DSPHandler and func_80336680.
Search for later movement of src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.h into include/dolphin/dsp.h or another shared Dolphin header location.
Check whether __DSPRegs and HW_REG conventions were reused in other Dolphin hardware modules.
Track later renames of __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_first_task, and __DSP_curr_task into typed task pointers or structs.
